programme.title: The Equalizer (2014)
programme.desc: A retired secret agent applies his talents to the task of helping victims of injustice. When he witnesses a prostitute being beaten up by a pimp, he kills the man, an act that draws him into a battle to the death with a Russian crime syndicate. Action thriller based on the 1980s TV series, starring Denzel Washington and Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: The Equalizer 2 (2018)
programme.desc: Robert McCall divides his time between cab driving and looking after a young man who is mixing with a bad crowd. When a CIA friend is murdered, the former black ops agent sets out to avenge her death, and winds up fighting for survival in his hurricane-battered home town. Action thriller, starring Denzel Washington, Pedro Pascal and Ashton Sanders

programme.title: Police Academy (1984)
programme.desc: An American police college lowers its entrance requirements in the hope of attracting new recruits, and in response, assorted dubious candidates turn up for enrolment. programme.title: Lethal Weapon (1987)
programme.desc: An unstable Vietnam veteran cop teams up with a by-the-book Los Angeles police detective to investigate the apparent suicide of the daughter of a wealthy businessman, leading them to a violent and bloody confrontation with a ruthless drug cartel under the control of a retired US general. Action thriller, starring Mel Gibson, Danny Glover, Gary Busey, Mitchell Ryan and Tom Atkins

programme.title: Warehouse 13
programme.desc: When a severe lightning-like blast disrupts the electricity supply to numerous homes and turns a man working on a billboard to dust, Myka finds the destruction familiar

programme.title: Warehouse 13
programme.desc: Petes ex-wife is stung by a metal bee from the warehouse with bizarre results, while Claudia and Steve search for an artefact at a Civil War re-enactment

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: As Carl slowly recovers from his operation, Daryl ventures alone into the woods to search for the boys missing friend Sophia. Dale and T-Dog get a shock as they collect water from a nearby well and a trip to the pharmacy for medical supplies ends unexpectedly for Glenn and Maggie. Back at the farm, Hershel grows impatient with the newcomers, while Lori suspects she may be pregnant

programme.title: Reginald the Vampire
programme.desc: The much-anticipated Assessment has finally arrived. The only open question is whether or not Reginald will survive it

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and the team visit Smithills Hall in Bolton where they encounter tales of religious persecution

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and a team of investigators visit a haunted museum in Cambridgeshire. Peterborough Museum was built in 1816 and is said to be haunted by First World War Australian soldier Sergeant Thomas Hunter

programme.title: Most Haunted

programme.desc: In Fleet, Hampshire, Stuart and his daughter Freya call upon Flo Headlam and Lee Burkhills help to create a relaxing family garden, incorporating separate, more private spaces. programme.title: Britains Almost Perfect Murders
programme.desc: The story of police officer Martin Forshaw, who killed his fiance, Claire Howarth, just days before their wedding. His poor staging of a car crash led to a life sentence.

programme.title: Murder UK
programme.desc: The case of Hollie Gazzard, a young hairdresser from the UK who was hoping for a fresh start but was tragically killed by her ex, leaving her family and community devastated.

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles tells the story of a young man kicked in the head by his girlfriends horse and the tale of survival of a British diver lost at sea off the Australian coast for more than 22 hours

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles tells the story of two girls who phoned 999 after their mother fell from the loft. Plus, a trucker who came face to face with another lorry as he drove around a bend

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A man goes to the aid of his friend when a great white shark attacks and a motorist risks her own life to save a family when their car bursts into flames following a motorway crash

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When a tortured artist is murdered, Father Brown must discover who committed the crime before it is too late. Starring Mark Williams and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The conclusion of a two-part story. Neville must solve a mystery from the past that unlocks the truth of who murdered renowned concert pianist Pasha Verdinikov and why. programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: TV presenters Alex Jones and Rick Edwards, comedian Greg Davies and performance poet John Cooper Clarke join forces with team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the panel show in which participants attempt to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts an extended edition of the quiz with a difference, beginning the K series with a range of fiendish questions on such topics as knees and knockers. Regular panellist Alan Davies is joined by Sara Pascoe, Jack Whitehall and David Mitchell, with points being awarded for interesting answers as well as correct ones

programme.title: Frankie Boyles New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian tackles the biggest issues troubling the planet, making a string of bold and often outrageous statements which hell then pick apart with the help of regular contributors Sara Pascoe, Katherine Ryan, Mona Chalabi and a variety of other guests, including comics and experts

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: David realises that his time apart from Patrick has gone on for too long and he looks for a way to reconcile. Moira pitches a Singles Week event at the Town Hall. Sitcom, starring Eugene Levy

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: Jocelyn bullies David into throwing her a last-minute baby shower, Alexis runs into an old friend, and Moira and Johnny do field research for the towns upcoming singles week. Sitcom, starring Eugene Levy

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: Moira tries to get out of jury duty and David attempts damage control for a Rose Apothecary product

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the history behind some of the countrys most popular spices and explore how they have been used to liven up meals. The culinary duo also visit a chilli festival - with mouth-numbing consequences

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: A Surrey police response unit is called to a stabbing incident at a teen house party in a captivating insight into teamwork, tactics and compassion

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: The National Crime Agency take on a people-trafficking group while Surrey and City of London Police hunt down exploitative romance fraudsters

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A locomotive needs a quick double bogie change to get back to the sugarcane harvest. Meanwhile, the heat is on for a tricky air conditioner replacement

programme.title: Rick Steins Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef heads across one of the busiest border crossings in the world - San Diego to Tijuana - for the start of his Mexican adventures. He follows the Pacific coastline down to Ensenada, and samples flour tortilla burritos, fresh fish, marinated and cooked on Popotla Beach, and visits the Valle de Guadalupe - Mexicos best kept secret for fabulous wines

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Border officers in Detroit apprehend a woman after finding a handgun and ammunition in her vehicle, and officers in Eagle Pass find $150,000-worth of methamphetamine

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers find a manmade compartment within the trailer of a truck returning Stateside, and two Americans arouse suspicions with an unsafe lane change at the US-Canada border

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorcycle racer and lorry mechanic attempts, with the aid of slipstreaming, to break the British record for outright speed on a bicycle - 110mph. Guy recruits a team comprising a truck racer, a bike builder and a design engineer to prepare for the feat, and undergoes a training regime with double Olympic cycling gold medallist Laura Trott before heading to Pendine Sands on the shores of Carmarthen Bay for his challenge

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May head to New Zealand to take part in a race along the coastline to the tip of the North Island - with one behind the wheel of a car and the other in an Americas Cup yacht. Back in the UK, Richard Hammond tries out three hot hatches as he puts the Renaultsport Clio 200, Peugeot 208 GTi and Ford Fiesta ST through their paces, and a host of stars including Warwick Davis, Charles Dance and Joss Stone assemble at the test track to welcome a new Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of the Ferrari F12 Berlinetta, sampling the punch of its 730bhp V12 engine on the open roads of Scotland. Richard Hammond harnesses the power of motorsport on a quest to find the worlds best taxi, and James May pays tribute to the former BBC Television Centre in White City, London, using a motorcyclist and a pair of parkour runners. Plus, the BAC Mono - an astonishingly fast car with just one seat - is put through its paces on the test track, and film director Ron Howard goes for a spin in the new Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how Britains love of cheese has evolved over centuries, travelling to Scotland to sample some of its finest varieties and discovering that the original cheesecake recipes were not American but British

programme.title: Rick Steins Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef lands in the town that placed Tequila on the map and explores Guadalajara, the city that gave the rest of the world Mariachis and dishes like carne con chilli. He also cant resist a visit to Casa Kimberly, bought by Richard Burton for Elizabeth Taylor, and where they still serve her favourite cocktail, a chocolate martini

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon introduces a seasonal special of the comedy panel game. TV presenter and poker whizz Victoria Coren Mitchell, BBC Breakfast anchor Naga Munchetty, Last Leg co-host Alex Brooker and veteran actor Melvyn Hayes join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with a few festive fibs or facts about themselves. So, did Victoria really once shock husband David with one of her Christmas presents to him? And did Alex once tip the bin men £500?

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Maisie Adam, Rory Bremner, James Cracknell and Michelle Gayle go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Charlie Brooks, Les Dennis, Darren Harriott and Melinda Messenger go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts an extended edition of the quiz with a difference, beginning the K series with a range of fiendish questions on such topics as knees and knockers. Regular panellist Alan Davies is joined by Sara Pascoe, Jack Whitehall and David Mitchell, with points being awarded for interesting answers as well as correct ones

programme.title: Frankie Boyles New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian dissects the weeks news using stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction. programme.desc: The chef heads across one of the busiest border crossings in the world - San Diego to Tijuana - for the start of his Mexican adventures. programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Border officers in Detroit apprehend a woman after finding a handgun and ammunition in her vehicle, and officers in Eagle Pass find $150,000-worth of methamphetamine

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorcycle racer and lorry mechanic attempts, with the aid of slipstreaming, to break the British record for outright speed on a bicycle - 110mph. Guy recruits a team comprising a truck racer, a bike builder and a design engineer to prepare for the feat, and undergoes a training regime with double Olympic cycling gold medallist Laura Trott before heading to Pendine Sands on the shores of Carmarthen Bay for his challenge

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May head to New Zealand to take part in a race along the coastline to the tip of the North Island - with one behind the wheel of a car and the other in an Americas Cup yacht. Back in the UK, Richard Hammond tries out three hot hatches as he puts the Renaultsport Clio 200, Peugeot 208 GTi and Ford Fiesta ST through their paces, and a host of stars including Warwick Davis, Charles Dance and Joss Stone assemble at the test track to welcome a new Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of the Ferrari F12 Berlinetta, sampling the punch of its 730bhp V12 engine on the open roads of Scotland. Richard Hammond harnesses the power of motorsport on a quest to find the worlds best taxi, and James May pays tribute to the former BBC Television Centre in White City, London, using a motorcyclist and a pair of parkour runners. Plus, the BAC Mono - an astonishingly fast car with just one seat - is put through its paces on the test track, and film director Ron Howard goes for a spin in the new Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: A stranger asks for help with a leaking car radiator shortly before police turn up at the house, but it transpires he has a hidden agenda. Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson star

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Roman Catholic priest spies trouble when Lady Felicia returns to Kembleford from Africa, having split from her lover Benedict Northam. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: A skeleton found buried in Edie Paigntons allotment is difficult for Sister Boniface to identify as male or female. Paigntons husband has disappeared, as has Frank Harsdens pregnant daughter Jenny. More tests by Sister Boniface reveal the victim had been murdered with a cultivator and buried in a caustic soda mixture.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a 1970s guitarist is investigated following the deathbed confession of a witness. Strickland, a fan of the rock stars band, reveals himself to be a keen musician, while Brian tracks down Jack and persuades him to rejoin Ucos. With guest appearances by Roger Lloyd Pack and George Costigan

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective struggles to cope following the death of his wife and suffers more grief when the body of his godson is discovered in a bricked-up drain 12 years after he went missing. Lynley himself becomes a suspect when the boys sister is found dead, forcing him to enlist the help of his lawyer and DS McCaffreys son to prove his innocence. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Nicholas Farrell

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty and amiable sidekick Geoffrey become embroiled in a convoluted case involving a deaf birdwatcher who goes missing at the same time as a womans body is found at the bottom of a cliff he is known to frequent. Light-hearted drama, with Patricia Routledge and Dominic Monaghan, featuring a guest appearance by David Bower

programme.title: Whitstable Pearl
programme.desc: A retired policeman is murdered during a Civil War re-enactment and the increasingly complicated investigation into his death uncovers three mistresses and a 35-year-old feud

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Top television spy series Operation QT is filming at the convent when a real bullet is fired instead of a replica, narrowly missing the leading actor. Working with DS Livingstone, Sister Boniface soon works out the real target was the shows producer, Dick Lansky, who has many enemies on and off set and a roving eye for the actresses. Lorna Watson stars

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien suffers a major dilemma - she wants to have children but is scared witless by the prospect of being pregnant. Meanwhile, Garth becomes a champion pancake tosser - much to his imprisoned fathers dismay. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Inspector Monroe clamps down on anti-social behaviour among the youth of Sun Hill, but others suffer as a result of his single-mindedness

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Nick and Jimmi come into conflict over the treatment of an agoraphobic mother who cannot come to the surgery. Melody discovers JJ is taking illegal drugs and Archie reveals the extent of his habit before telling Eva where she can find him

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: When her mother dies, an old school friend of Ruths asks for help in tracking down the man she thinks may be her father. Archie bumps into Melody and is racked with guilt about his conversation with Eva regarding JJ, and George persuades Ronnie to agree to invite Daniel over for dinner

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric turns 50 so the staff throw a surprise party for him - but tensions end up running high and many relationships are put to the test as Jess tries to make Zubin jealous, Tricia attempts to seduce Carlos, and Connie enjoys playing mind games with Will. Meanwhile, Diane cant decide whether to accept Owens offer, and Chrissie reminds her how unreliable he can be

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruth is distraught to learn she has been sectioned, and resolves to escape from the ward where she is being detained. However, as she talks to consultant psychiatrist Andrew Brookfield (Cold Bloods Matthew Kelly) about a former patient suspected of having Munchausens syndrome, it becomes apparent the case could hold the key to her mental health problems. Starring Georgia Taylor

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Inspector Monroe clamps down on anti-social behaviour among the youth of Sun Hill, but others suffer as a result of his single-mindedness

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Zainabs deceit is finally revealed to a devastated Masood. Pat and Peggy each show off their new gentleman friend to a bewildered Dot

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Zainab fights to save her marriage as Tamwar makes a confession. Billy resorts to desperate measures to make ends meet and the identity of the Blue Masquerader is revealed

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A confectioners beloved cat falls victim to a mystery illness that defies all Jamess attempts to find a cure. Rural drama, with Robert Hardy, Christopher Timothy and Geoffrey Bayldon

programme.title: Howards Way
programme.desc: Avril confronts Ken and Lynne returns home. Meanwhile, Sir Edward reveals shocking news about his health

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The owners of a Tuscan inn are sent a severed finger and a photo of their son. Convinced he has been kidnapped by the Mafia, their daughter hires Hetty while she and Robert are on holiday

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: An encounter with an old flame makes Sharon feel broody, while Tracey has her work cut out pampering Garth in the school holidays. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy feels the need to do something special for the Queens Silver Jubilee so he volunteers himself, Clegg, and Compo to help with the towns pageant. However, the need arises to find someone capable of driving a tractor. Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg attends the funeral of an old friend, and later tries to convince Foggy and Compo that flowers have emotions and that they react to vocal tone and music. Compos attention is more focused on his own needs, in particular enacting a new plan to drive Nora Batty into his arms. Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brain Wilde star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Who Made a Bit of a Splash in Wales Then?
programme.desc: Compo and Clegg gatecrash Foggys trip to see a female friend in Wales. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Queens of Mystery
programme.desc: A second death at the Wildemarsh Wellness Retreat leads Mattie to uncover a heart-wrenching tragedy, waiting to be avenged

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mallory is forced to turn to the sleuthing priest for help when a senior member of the police force is murdered at a bowls match. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens a case originally worked on by a corrupt colleague of Sandras father, leading the detective to seek the truth about her familys shady past - a decision that puts pressure on the relationship with her mother. Brian investigates a suspicious death at a brewery, while Gerry and Jack take the time to sample its wares

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A diabetic girl disappears without her insulin, and Lynley discovers she was last seen talking to a couple who had previously been sighted with a youngster later found drowned in a lake. The detective stages a press appeal, hoping to learn more about the mysterious pair and reveal their roles in both cases. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The owners of a Tuscan inn are sent a severed finger and a photo of their son. Convinced he has been kidnapped by the Mafia, their daughter hires Hetty while she and Robert are on holiday

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The Brokenwood fountain is spraying reddish-brown water due to a man impaled on its sculpture; the victim is an American tourist who has three gold coins

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part five of seven. The Brigadier summons the Doctor and Liz to his research centre after several of his staff suffer nervous breakdowns

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part six of seven. While the Doctor and Liz try to find a cure to the virus spread by Baker, the young Silurian seizes control of his people.

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Its a race against time to dig out a man buried under three tonnes of soil, and the team are called to rescue the victims of a major crash involving a taxi

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: The historian examines four dramatic chapters in British history, exploring how changing attitudes to gender politics, class inequality, mental health and children can challenge perceptions of the past and provide new answers to each mystery. She begins with the 16th-century witch hunts, when thousands of ordinary people, mostly women, were hunted down, tortured and killed across Scotland and England

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: The historian examines Britains deadliest pandemic, exploring how its monumental death toll changed the face of the country for ever and what the sudden loss of life meant for the church and religious beliefs, landowners and for the those who survived. Lucy also learns how DNA extracted from the skeletons of the plagues victims enabled scientists to finally identify a bacteria called Yersinia Pestis - a pathogen to which the mediaeval population had no immunity

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy uncovers the story of Edward IVs sons Edward and Richard, whose disappearance in 1483 has led to centuries of mystery. The princes were taken to the Tower of London for their own protection, but a priest declared the boys illegitimate and their uncle Richard next in line to the throne. The gaps in the historical record have fuelled 500 years of speculation, so Lucy speaks to history professor Tim Thornton, who has found evidence of one account of what happened written by Thomas More, while Richard III Society chair Matthew Lewis gives his views on the events

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates how George IIIs mental illness changed Britain, uncovering royal papers and exploring how the attempt on the kings life by a mentally ill subject transformed psychiatry forever

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: The historian examines four dramatic chapters in British history, exploring how changing attitudes to gender politics, class inequality, mental health and children can challenge perceptions of the past and provide new answers to each mystery. She begins with the 16th-century witch hunts, when thousands of ordinary people, mostly women, were hunted down, tortured and killed across Scotland and England

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Lindisfarne, which lies off the Northumberland coast. From what became known as the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, monks spread Christianity to the rest of northern England and created one of Britains finest treasures - the Lindisfarne Gospels. Viking attacks caused the monks to flee but the village survived and today the community has to share it with thousands of tourists

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Man and Beast with Martin Clunes
programme.desc: The comedy actor sets out on an international journey to investigate the extraordinary relationship between man and beast. In the first of two episodes, Martin travels to Nepal, where he meets a farmer whose cows not only supply his income from milk, but also provide the fuel for lighting and cooking in the family home

programme.title: Man and Beast with Martin Clunes
programme.desc: The actor concludes his international exploration into the relationship between man and beast. In the second of two programmes, he meets doting dog owners in Tokyo, learns the truth behind the outlawed practice of snake-charming in Nepal and find out how small birds in Thailand are used for gambling. Back in Britain, he partakes in a traditional swan-upping event on the River Thames to monitor the population of the birds, and discovers how horses are used to help autistic children

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of Australia
programme.desc: With more than 8,000 islands to choose from, Martin Clunes visits 16 which provide an insight into the diversity, history and challenges of island life in Australia. His first stop is Lord Howe Island, where the pace of life is slow and the population is a steady 350. He also visits Norfolk Island, Australias most easterly territory, and the remotest island on Martins journey

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of Australia
programme.desc: The actor travels to the Tiwi Islands, swims with a whale shark, and visits the Houtman Abrolhos, a string of over 100 little islands. Martin Clunes also samples life on Rottnest Island, which has long been a playground retreat for mainland visitors

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Dramatised documentary examining how Hitler and the Nazi party rose to power in Germany during the 1930s, revealing the greed, manipulation and frightening underestimation of Adolf Hitlers ambition. In 1930, Germany was a liberal democracy, with elections, parliament and the rule of law, but just four years later its leader was a dictator and its government is in the hands of murderers. Leading historians and experts take viewers inside the minds of those hellbent on gaining power and those who tried to stop them. First broadcast in 1997

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.sub-title: The First Six Months in Power
programme.desc: It is 1933 and Adolf Hitler is the chancellor of Germany, but he does not have absolute power as there is still a democratic parliament, a head of state and the rule of law. Hitler calls a general election to increase Nazi representation, but a fire in the Reichstag building is blamed on communists - prompting thousands of arrests of left-wingers. Hitler then calls on President von Hindenburg to act and a decree is passed giving the Nazis emergency powers to ban free speech and the right to protest

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The final episode examines the infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler - urged on by Hermann Goering and Heinrich Himmler - ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat to the Nazi regime. The most well-known of these was Ernst Roehm, commander and co-founder of the Sturmabteilung, the Nazi Partys militia, whom Hitler had come to see as a potential rival. At least 85 people died during the purge, although the final death toll may have been in the hundreds

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of seven. The Doctor investigates a research centre where monsters have been sighted in nearby caves. Guest starring Fulton Mackay (Porridge) and Paul Darrow (Blakes 7)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of seven. The Doctor leads an expedition into the caves in search of the creatures. Captain Hawkins fires on a mysterious humanoid figure, who flees to the surface

programme.desc: Part three of seven. The Silurians contact Quinn for help retrieving the cohort, and the Doctor and the Brigadier organise a search of the moors.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part four of seven. When Quinn turns up dead, the Brigadier leads an armed party into the caves. Meanwhile, the Doctor tries to reach out to the Silurians

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part six of seven. While the Doctor and Liz try to find a cure to the virus spread by Baker, the young Silurian seizes control of his people.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part seven of seven. While Liz neutralises the threat of the virus, the Doctor learns of an even more devastating Silurian plan.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Dramatised documentary examining how Hitler and the Nazi party rose to power in Germany during the 1930s, revealing the greed, manipulation and frightening underestimation of Adolf Hitlers ambition. In 1930, Germany was a liberal democracy, with elections, parliament and the rule of law, but just four years later its leader was a dictator and its government is in the hands of murderers. Leading historians and experts take viewers inside the minds of those hellbent on gaining power and those who tried to stop them. First broadcast in 1997

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.sub-title: The First Six Months in Power
programme.desc: It is 1933 and Adolf Hitler is the chancellor of Germany, but he does not have absolute power as there is still a democratic parliament, a head of state and the rule of law. Hitler calls a general election to increase Nazi representation, but a fire in the Reichstag building is blamed on communists - prompting thousands of arrests of left-wingers. Hitler then calls on President von Hindenburg to act and a decree is passed giving the Nazis emergency powers to ban free speech and the right to protest

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The final episode examines the infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler - urged on by Hermann Goering and Heinrich Himmler - ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat to the Nazi regime. The most well-known of these was Ernst Roehm, commander and co-founder of the Sturmabteilung, the Nazi Partys militia, whom Hitler had come to see as a potential rival. At least 85 people died during the purge, although the final death toll may have been in the hundreds

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Louis spends time with the global pop star Rita Ora. Louis meets Rita at her dads pub in West London where they sit down for a revealing conversation that takes in her rise to stardom, her familys arrival in the UK in 1990, and the difficulties she has faced dealing with intense press scrutiny and speculation about her personal life. Later, Louis spends time with Rita on the set of her latest music video in LA, before joining her as she puts on a spectacular live performance in Albania

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Today in Stoke, paramedics Mick and Rachel help a young man who has hurt his back. In Dudley, paramedics help a 74-year-old who had a shock from his internal defibrillator

programme.sub-title: Terri, Esme and the Chi Family

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS at Great Ormond Street Hospital
programme.desc: The DIY SOS team are out of their comfort zone this week, joining forces with two world-famous institutions - Great Ormond Street Hospital and the Chelsea Flower Show - for a special challenge. Gardener Chris Beardshaw has promised his gold medal-winning garden from the flower show to the hospital - now all Nick Knowles and his fellow builders have to do is crane the display across London and rebuild it on the hospital. Its a hugely ambitious build, with tricky logistics and emotional volunteers. But knowing how much the garden will prove welcome respite for families, the team is determined not to fail

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles, his team and a group of volunteers are in Monmouth, Wales, to transform the family home of Charlotte, a mother recovering from a stroke that left her with brain damage, speech loss and partial paralysis. The current layout of her home makes it difficult for Charlotte to move around and have her physiotherapy treatment, so the team redesign the house to make things easier for her, her husband Rob and their children

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to the Isle of Sheppey in Kent to meet two extraordinary dads, Garry and Kyle, who have adopted four vulnerable children - three of whom have special needs - to give them the best life possible. But their cramped three-bedroom bungalow is making family life difficult. So to give this family the space they so desperately need, the DIY SOS builders are undertaking their most ambitious project yet - to demolish the bungalow and build a bespoke five-bedroom house in its place. But with just nine days to complete the task, it looks like being their toughest one yet

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Staffordshire bull terrier requires attention when it suffers complete paralysis in its back legs, and a Devon rex cat is brought in after being struck by a car. Its eight-year-old owner faces a tough decision when X-rays reveal a severely broken pelvis and a ruptured bladder. Plus, a young cavalier king has had seven operations in a bid to fix its knee joint, and Noel tries to resolve the problem

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick pushes the boundaries of veterinary medicine, and treats a German shepherd puppy that needs surgery on his hips and front leg using groundbreaking cell treatment. The vet must also perform delicate surgery on a chihuahua suffering from a neck deformity compressing its spinal cord - however, one false move could be fatal. Plus, a rescue dog with deformed front legs is brought into the surgery

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS at Great Ormond Street Hospital

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team joins the search for a missing aeroplane pilot and passenger. A man digging for antique bottles in a South Yorkshire tip is buried alive, leading to a desperate fight to save him

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: A scientists body is found in Loch Katrine, a case that reminds Annika of a play by Ibsen. But with so many people wanting the victim dead, which one did he push over the edge? Away from work, the DI takes more than a parental interest in Morgans therapist. Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: The team are called in when writer Cara Gibson is found dead under a bridge. Her books ruined careers and left a lot of lives in tatters, but could any of her subjects be driven to murder? Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: The junior doctors are now in their second week at the Royal Berkshire Hospital. Adrian does a tricky blood test and Tej spots some worrying test results

programme.title: Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Katie meets Candice, who is facing a second-degree murder charge, and learns that she was born into a gang and has grown up around violence

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help the wife of an American football fan who has died but asked for help cleaning his man cave before his passing

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programme.desc: The rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn, footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse, including a leading Florida State Senator, Lauren Book, the UK campaigner Kate Isaacs, and Dina, a woman who was deep-faked by a work colleague

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets midwife and kidney patient Sascha, who has to undergo intense dialysis sessions for hours on end, five days a week. This places the mother-of-fours body under great stress and the constant threat of blood clots gives her a life expectancy of just a few years. Determined to help, the DIY SOS team, bolstered by the local tradespeople and volunteers of Welwyn Garden City, renovate her home to include a dedicated dialysis room, offering her the chance of many more years with her family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team return to Veterans Street in Manchester, where, in 2015, with the help of Prince William and Prince Harry, DIY SOS launched their most ambitious project yet - transforming a derelict street into a vibrant community for veterans and local people. This time they build the final home for decorated former soldier and single dad Simon Flores, whose foot was blown off by a roadside bomb during a patrol in Iraq

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team helps a couple whose 24-year-old son Ryan has been living in hospital for the last five years, as they renovate the downstairs of their three-bedroom house in Bristol so he can move back home. The house needs a downstairs extension with all the bespoke care provision that Ryan will need, including physio space, a wet-room, a family area and the ability to access the entire ground floor, and if the family doesnt get this work done, Ryan will remain in hospital 50 miles away

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Festive edition of the documentary from 2015 following the work of Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team. A sleigh-pulling Alaskan malamute needs both knees replacing with implants, but his owners are forced to make a difficult decision as he recovers from his first operation. Meanwhile, a springer spaniel is rushed into the surgery with a shattered jaw, so the vets decide to try something new to get him home in time for Christmas. Plus, a miniature dachshund with a deformed foot is given intensive physiotherapy, and Noel gets a surprise visitor

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick performs a risky procedure to save the life of an 18-week-old cockapoo puppy that has fractured its neck following an accident in the garden. He also comes to the aid of Romanian stray Geoffrey, who was found abandoned and emaciated before being adopted by Kent couple John and Stacey, and an accident-prone cat called MK who has fallen from a window

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets midwife and kidney patient Sascha, who has to undergo intense dialysis sessions for hours on end, five days a week. This places the mother-of-fours body under great stress and the constant threat of blood clots gives her a life expectancy of just a few years. This time they build the final home for decorated former soldier and single dad Simon Flores, whose foot was blown off by a roadside bomb during a patrol in Iraq

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing and TOWIE star Pete Wicks immerses himself in the heart-warming and emotional world of dog care at Basildons Dogs Trust rehoming centre. Pete returns to Dogs Trust, facing a suitcase of abandoned pups on day one and falling hard for Theo, a gentle Italian Mastiff with a serious heart condition.

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Three more expectant mothers visit Leeds maternity wards. Katie, 21, and Jonathan, 22, were not together when she found out she was pregnant, but they were reunited 12 weeks later at the first scan, and at that moment Jonathan realised he wanted to be a good father. Meanwhile, 20-year-old Nicole is admitted with a significant bleed, and the midwives race to fill the bath for 30-year-old Jolanta, who has experienced quick labours in the past and wants a water birth for her third baby

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: The documentary follows two expectant mothers whose unplanned pregnancies happened at very different ages. Eighteen-year-old Laura, who has developed gestational diabetes, is booked in for an early induction, but shes rushed to theatre when her babys heartbeat drops. Meanwhile, mum-of-two Nina, 39, and Andy, 41, await a Caesareancaesareanand the arrival of their twins

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The Shipmans drive to Wales for the wedding fayre, and after a momentous trip to the church for the reading of the banns, Nessa tells Stacey her big secret. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne and Joanna Page

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The bride-to-be goes on a hen party, while Gavin embarks on two stag nights, the second of which reveals shocking secrets about his fiancee that could jeopardise the wedding. Elsewhere, the early return of Staceys brother Jason horrifies Uncle Bryn. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne and Joanna Page

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Its the night shift for the junior doctors. Adrians ego takes a hit as he bites off more than he can chew, and Ese must solve the mystery of a patients unexplained collapse

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis interviews some of the biggest names in UK entertainment and is granted exclusive access into their lives at home and at work, beginning with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south-west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life; from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, and the pressures that come with being one of the countrys best-loved musicians and role models

programme.title: Dangerous Breed: Crime. Cons. Cats.
programme.desc: Cat-breeding wrestler Teddy Hart teams up with filmmaker Frederick Kroetsch for a reality show, but things take a dark turn when two women make disturbing allegations

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Katie meets Candice, who is facing a second-degree murder charge, and learns that she was born into a gang and has grown up around violence

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Dougs fiancee wont move in until he clears his tiki collection and his dead ex-wifes things, so the Death Cleaners help him sort out the clutter in his heart and home

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Louis spends time with the global pop star Rita Ora. Louis meets Rita at her dads pub in West London where they sit down for a revealing conversation that takes in her rise to stardom, her familys arrival in the UK in 1990, and the difficulties she has faced dealing with intense press scrutiny and speculation about her personal life. Later, Louis spends time with Rita on the set of her latest music video in LA, before joining her as she puts on a spectacular live performance in Albania

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Today in Stoke, paramedics Mick and Rachel help a young man who has hurt his back. In Dudley, paramedics help a 74-year-old who had a shock from his internal defibrillator

programme.desc: Pete meets Lurcher Terri, whos terrified of the world and must learn to trust again, and helps settle a family of chihuahuas given up by their heartbroken owner

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS at Great Ormond Street Hospital
programme.desc: The DIY SOS team are out of their comfort zone this week, joining forces with two world-famous institutions - Great Ormond Street Hospital and the Chelsea Flower Show - for a special challenge. Gardener Chris Beardshaw has promised his gold medal-winning garden from the flower show to the hospital - now all Nick Knowles and his fellow builders have to do is crane the display across London and rebuild it on the hospital. Its a hugely ambitious build, with tricky logistics and emotional volunteers. But knowing how much the garden will prove welcome respite for families, the team is determined not to fail

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles, his team and a group of volunteers are in Monmouth, Wales, to transform the family home of Charlotte, a mother recovering from a stroke that left her with brain damage, speech loss and partial paralysis. The current layout of her home makes it difficult for Charlotte to move around and have her physiotherapy treatment, so the team redesign the house to make things easier for her, her husband Rob and their children

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to the Isle of Sheppey in Kent to meet two extraordinary dads, Garry and Kyle, who have adopted four vulnerable children - three of whom have special needs - to give them the best life possible. But their cramped three-bedroom bungalow is making family life difficult. So to give this family the space they so desperately need, the DIY SOS builders are undertaking their most ambitious project yet - to demolish the bungalow and build a bespoke five-bedroom house in its place. But with just nine days to complete the task, it looks like being their toughest one yet

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Staffordshire bull terrier requires attention when it suffers complete paralysis in its back legs, and a Devon rex cat is brought in after being struck by a car. Its eight-year-old owner faces a tough decision when X-rays reveal a severely broken pelvis and a ruptured bladder. Plus, a young cavalier king has had seven operations in a bid to fix its knee joint, and Noel tries to resolve the problem

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick pushes the boundaries of veterinary medicine, and treats a German shepherd puppy that needs surgery on his hips and front leg using groundbreaking cell treatment. The vet must also perform delicate surgery on a chihuahua suffering from a neck deformity compressing its spinal cord - however, one false move could be fatal. Plus, a rescue dog with deformed front legs is brought into the surgery

programme.title: DIY SOS

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team joins the search for a missing aeroplane pilot and passenger. A man digging for antique bottles in a South Yorkshire tip is buried alive, leading to a desperate fight to save him

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: A scientists body is found in Loch Katrine, a case that reminds Annika of a play by Ibsen. But with so many people wanting the victim dead, which one did he push over the edge? Away from work, the DI takes more than a parental interest in Morgans therapist. Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: The team are called in when writer Cara Gibson is found dead under a bridge. Her books ruined careers and left a lot of lives in tatters, but could any of her subjects be driven to murder? Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker

programme.title: Searching for Satoshi: The Mysterious Disappearance of the Bitcoin Creator
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the secretive character known by the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto, who launched the cryptocurrenty Bitcoin - and then disappeared

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Its the night shift for the junior doctors. Adrians ego takes a hit as he bites off more than he can chew, and Ese must solve the mystery of a patients unexplained collapse

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help the wife of an American football fan who has died but asked for help cleaning his man cave before his passing

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programme.desc: The rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn, footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse, including a leading Florida State Senator, Lauren Book, the UK campaigner Kate Isaacs, and Dina, a woman who was deep-faked by a work colleague

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: The junior doctors are now in their second week at the Royal Berkshire Hospital. Adrian does a tricky blood test and Tej spots some worrying test results

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The day of the wedding arrives and Bryn is still behaving oddly around Staceys brother. Nessa debates whether to share her big secret and Smithy resolves to give a superb best mans speech - but finds himself overcome with emotion under the spotlight. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Oscar-winning actress at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre, her life away from acting and her relationship with her husband, the late Michael Williams. Louis also accompanies Judi to the Gielgud Theatre in Londons West End, where they discuss the difficulties of the lockdown and how it inadvertently led to her surprise popularity on TikTok

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis interviews some of the biggest names in UK entertainment and is granted exclusive access into their lives at home and at work, beginning with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team set out on a pre-war challenge with a 1939 three-wheeled Morgan F4 - and aptly named Russ Storer from MG Mecca is still keen to take it on

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave travels to leafy Leicestershire where Steve Perry has a 1980 Ford Granada Ghia and a 1981 Ford Cortina

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien travel Britain searching for items to fix up and flog for their owners. In East Sussex and Hertfordshire, the duos finds include a Victorian egg incubator

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: On the evening of December 3, 1926, Agatha Christie left her home in Sunningdale, Berkshire, in her Morris Cowley. Ten days later, Agatha was discovered in a hotel in Harrogate, claiming to have lost her memory. Lucy Worsley delves into this traumatic, watershed moment in Christies life. She visits the key locations, from the chalk pit where Agatha crashed her car to Abney Hall, the grand house where she took refuge (and which would inspire several of her books)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Wakefield, West Yorkshire, Michael Portillo visits the National Coal Mining Museum for England at Caphouse Colliery. In Leeds, he heads for the Chapeltown area to investigate the origins of the Leeds West Indian carnival in 1967 and tries his hand on the steel drums

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his post-war exploration of northwest England in Bradford, Shipley and Hebden Bridge. In Bradfords Centenary Square, he encounters local literary giant, JB Priestley. At Shipley station, Michael discovers a nature reserve in the middle of the car park. Finally the Calder Valley Line delivers the presenter to the photogenic station at Hebden Bridge

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Londons Docklands to the Barbican, examining the story of the arrival at Tilbury in 1948 of the Empire Windrush, which brought more than a thousand passengers from the Caribbean to work in Britain. In Limehouse, Michael reflects on the Labour Partys landslide victory at the post-war general election of 1945 and hears what drove new prime minister Clement Attlee to strive for social change

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits several of Londons most famous locations, beginning on the South Bank, where the 1951 Festival of Britain drew more than eight and a half million visitors to admire fantastical buildings designed to inspire and celebrate the best of British in art, science and industry. After a spin on the London Eye, built to celebrate the millennium, Michael takes the London Underground to Chelsea to find out about Elizabeth David, one of the most influential cookery writers of the 20th century, and also heads to Covent Garden and Regents Park

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave collects a Ford Fiesta XR2i thats not moved from a garage for 21 years and Derek picks up a 1956 Siva Tourer, made famous in Doctor Who

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave meets the inspirational owner of a Reliant Robin whos selling it to raise money for charity

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates this documentary chronicling events leading up to the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbour on December 7, 1941, and the subsequent collapse of unprepared British forces before the advancing enemy in Singapore. Includes interviews with veterans from both sides

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: The story of a bear that fought alongside the Polish army, which became a terrifying modern myth among the German army during the Second World War

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition paying tribute to the nursing profession, filmed at St Thomass and St Bartholomews hospitals in the heart of London. The programme features objects that provide an insight into the history of nursing and healthcare, from medals awarded for bravery to early uniforms, cartoons and medical equipment. Fiona also meets comedian Jo Brand, a former psychiatric nurse, and hears the story of Jennifer Worth - the author of Call the Midwife

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an episode filmed before the Accession, featuring the Queen Consorts visit to the Eden Project to discuss charity project The Big Lunch. Silver specialist Duncan Campbell takes a look at a rare snuffbox from the Royal Collection that was made from Cornish silver, while books expert Justin Croft admires a copy of Grays Elegy in a Country Churchyard

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A 1969 Morris Mini Cooper MkII is totally restored by specialists. They give it the full works, which means only one thing... any chance of profit heads towards an open window

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team set out on a pre-war challenge with a 1939 three-wheeled Morgan F4 - and aptly named Russ Storer from MG Mecca is still keen to take it on

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates this documentary chronicling events leading up to the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbour on December 7, 1941, and the subsequent collapse of unprepared British forces before the advancing enemy in Singapore. Includes interviews with veterans from both sides

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: The story of a bear that fought alongside the Polish army, which became a terrifying modern myth among the German army during the Second World War

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition paying tribute to the nursing profession, filmed at St Thomass and St Bartholomews hospitals in the heart of London. The programme features objects that provide an insight into the history of nursing and healthcare, from medals awarded for bravery to early uniforms, cartoons and medical equipment. Fiona also meets comedian Jo Brand, a former psychiatric nurse, and hears the story of Jennifer Worth - the author of Call the Midwife

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The final leg of the journey takes in Morocco, where Simon visits a land borders between Africa and the European Union, and the coast of Spain, taking in the scale of the countrys fruit and vegetable industry. He discovers why tourists are actively discouraged from visiting Corsica, and goes diving in waters that have the highest fish population in the Mediterranean

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Expert hauliers Eric Harrison and Chris Case move an iconic Deltic diesel, better known as The Royal Highland Fusilier, halfway across the country to the Telford Steam Fair

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor operates one of the worlds biggest hydraulic cranes, gets behind the wheel of a tank, and discovers the story behind the invention of the hovercraft

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A 1969 Morris Mini Cooper MkII is totally restored by specialists. They give it the full works, which means only one thing... any chance of profit heads towards an open window

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek picks out a super low mileage MGC and the team pays a little too much to secure it

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave collects a Ford Fiesta XR2i thats not moved from a garage for 21 years and Derek picks up a 1956 Siva Tourer, made famous in Doctor Who

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien travel to the West Midlands and London, where finds include a potato oven and an old petrol pump

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: In 1930, recovering from a personal crisis, Agatha Christie fell in love with and married Max Mallowan. This relationship, and the security it brought, saw Agatha enter the most prolific and successful chapter of her career. This would be the period in which Agatha penned some of her most famous and enduring bestsellers, including Murder on the Orient Express. Lucy follows in Agathas footsteps to discover the roots of these works and explores how, in later life, Agatha achieved global celebrity - but remained very much the anonymous observer, hiding in plain sight

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures deep underground onto Londons newest railway - the Elizabeth Line - enjoying a smooth ride from Paddington to Tottenham Court Road. Emerging into the sunshine in Bedford Square, Michael recalls the choking smogs which plagued the capital during the 1950s. He learns how smoke-free zones were introduced to tackle the problem and, high above the capital at the top of the BT Tower, discovers how the capitals air quality is monitored today

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins the latest leg on the seafront at Felixstowe, where in January 1953 the town was engulfed in the worst flooding to hit England in the 20th century. In Norwich, he admires the Norman Cathedral, then traces the history of the postcode

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: At RAF Lakenheath, Michael Portillo discovers a slice of America dropped into the British countryside. In the market town of Brandon on the border between Norfolk and Suffolk, he visits Quorn Foods to find how a seemingly modern meat substitute emerged out of a post-war food crisis. Michael ends his journey in Cambridge, where he studied during the 1970s

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins a journey from the Midlands to the West Country, beginning in Derby, where he visits the citys famous 19th-century railway works. In Leicester he looks back to the early 1970s, when around 10,000 Asians arrived in the city after being expelled from Uganda by the dictator Idi Amin. He also hears how meat rationing during the Second World War led to the birth of Walkers crisps, still produced in the city

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave Mathewson has put his best clean T-shirt on to collect a 1965 Land Rover from a stately home - and it turns out that the trusty car has a real pedigree

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Pauls on the East Coast to help a collector who is saying goodbye to his prized Excelsior Consort and his handmade Yorkshire Coble boat

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Factors influencing Americas decision to support Britain during the Second World War, and how Hitlers declaration of war on the US convinced Congress to take more decisive action. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring the motivations of key figures in Hitlers regime, beginning with propaganda master Josef Goebbels

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the grounds of Belmont House in Kent, where she encounters a disguised Michael McIntyre preparing a prank for his Saturday night show. Items brought in to be valued include an early David Hockney painting, a trophy awarded to Take That in 1995, a sword that once belonged to a member of the Dervish religious order, and a telegram announcing the end of the Second World War

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Clissold Park in Stoke Newington. Treasures include a collection of ancient Peruvian pottery, with some pieces made over a thousand years ago, an archive of textile designs by pioneering post-war designer Althea McNish, and Harold Wilsons pipe. Ceramics expert Serhat Ahmet challenges Fiona to work out what she should buy, sell or keep from a selection of figures by the Meissen factory

programme.desc: Derek brings a small army of military hardware to Thornton le Dale including a Studebaker Weasel tracked vehicle and a WWII-era Willys Jeep

programme.desc: The team dive into the wartime history books and uncover a Harley Davidson 42WLA

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave Mathewson has put his best clean T-shirt on to collect a 1965 Land Rover from a stately home - and it turns out that the trusty car has a real pedigree

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien come across a water trough and a cartwheel as they continue their mission to turn unwanted items into hard cash

programme.title: American Historys Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy explores how American history has been mythologised and manipulated by generations of politicians, writers and protesters. She begins by examining the American Revolution - a David-and-Goliath battle of men with high ideals taking on the might of the British Empire. But how much of Americas founding story is based on fact?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave collects a Ford Fiesta XR2i thats not moved from a garage for 21 years and Derek picks up a 1956 Siva Tourer, made famous in Doctor Who

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien travel Britain searching for items to fix up and flog for their owners. In East Sussex and Hertfordshire, the duos finds include a Victorian egg incubator

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: On the evening of December 3, 1926, Agatha Christie left her home in Sunningdale, Berkshire, in her Morris Cowley. Ten days later, Agatha was discovered in a hotel in Harrogate, claiming to have lost her memory. Lucy Worsley delves into this traumatic, watershed moment in Christies life. She visits the key locations, from the chalk pit where Agatha crashed her car to Abney Hall, the grand house where she took refuge (and which would inspire several of her books)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Wakefield, West Yorkshire, Michael Portillo visits the National Coal Mining Museum for England at Caphouse Colliery. In Leeds, he heads for the Chapeltown area to investigate the origins of the Leeds West Indian carnival in 1967 and tries his hand on the steel drums

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his post-war exploration of northwest England in Bradford, Shipley and Hebden Bridge. In Bradfords Centenary Square, he encounters local literary giant, JB Priestley. At Shipley station, Michael discovers a nature reserve in the middle of the car park. Finally the Calder Valley Line delivers the presenter to the photogenic station at Hebden Bridge

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Londons Docklands to the Barbican, examining the story of the arrival at Tilbury in 1948 of the Empire Windrush, which brought more than a thousand passengers from the Caribbean to work in Britain. In Limehouse, Michael reflects on the Labour Partys landslide victory at the post-war general election of 1945 and hears what drove new prime minister Clement Attlee to strive for social change

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits several of Londons most famous locations, beginning on the South Bank, where the 1951 Festival of Britain drew more than eight and a half million visitors to admire fantastical buildings designed to inspire and celebrate the best of British in art, science and industry. After a spin on the London Eye, built to celebrate the millennium, Michael takes the London Underground to Chelsea to find out about Elizabeth David, one of the most influential cookery writers of the 20th century, and also heads to Covent Garden and Regents Park

programme.desc: Dave collects a Ford Fiesta XR2i thats not moved from a garage for 21 years and Derek picks up a 1956 Siva Tourer, made famous in Doctor Who

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave is off to London where a couple of very special Fords, including a pristine Escort Cosworth, are hidden away in a garage

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien come across a water trough and a cartwheel as they continue their mission to turn unwanted items into hard cash
